# MetaGenesis Core

Open verification protocol for computational claims.
Implements the MetaGenesis Verification Protocol (MVP) v0.1.

Any computational result (ML accuracy, calibration, data pipeline output,
risk model output) can be packaged into a self-contained verification bundle.
A third party runs one command and gets PASS or FAIL with a reason.
Semantic verification catches tampering that survives SHA-256.

NOT a simulation platform. NOT an AI system. NOT a modeling tool.
A governance + evidence + verification pipeline. An open protocol.

## What this repo does

1. Wraps any computational result in a tamper-evident evidence bundle
2. Verifies bundles offline with integrity + semantic layers
3. Enforces governance: every claim has implementation, every implementation has claim
4. Detects drift against verified calibration anchors

## Key files to read first

- README.md — problem, solution, quickstart
- docs/PROTOCOL.md — MVP v0.1 protocol specification
- docs/USE_CASES.md — 5 target verticals with concrete examples
- docs/ROADMAP.md — planned expansion
- reports/scientific_claim_index.md — all active claims, job_kinds, thresholds
- reports/canonical_state.md — authoritative project state, active claims list
- reports/known_faults.yaml — known limitations, do not overclaim
- backend/progress/runner.py — job dispatch (_execute_job_logic)
- scripts/steward_audit.py — governance enforcement (run this to check integrity)
- scripts/mg.py — pack build and verify
- demos/open_data_demo_01/run_demo.py — working end-to-end demo

## Active claims (8)

MTR-1        mtr1_youngs_modulus_calibration          (materials science)
MTR-2        mtr2_thermal_paste_conductivity_calibration
MTR-3        mtr3_thermal_multilayer_contact_calibration
SYSID-01     sysid1_arx_calibration                   (system identification)
DATA-PIPE-01 datapipe1_quality_certificate             (data pipelines)
DRIFT-01     drift_calibration_monitor                 (drift detection)
ML_BENCH-01  mlbench1_accuracy_certificate             (ML benchmarking)
DT-FEM-01    dtfem1_displacement_verification          (digital twin / FEM)

## Governance invariant

runner dispatch kinds == scientific_claim_index job_kinds == canonical_state claims
Enforced by: python scripts/steward_audit.py → must output STEWARD AUDIT: PASS

## Hard rules (never violate)

- Never use "tamper-proof" — use "tamper-evident"
- Never invent implementations — if file not in repo, say so
- Never add a claim without: implementation + test + claim_index entry + canonical_state update
- steward_audit.py must PASS after every change
- known_faults.yaml is authoritative — do not contradict it

## Patent

USPTO PPA #63/996,819, filed 2026-03-05, inventor Yehor Bazhynov
4 patentable innovations: bidirectional claim coverage, semantic tamper
detection, policy-gate immutable anchors, dual-mode canary pipeline

## How to verify everything works

python scripts/steward_audit.py                              # PASS
python -m pytest tests/steward tests/materials tests/ml tests/systems tests/data tests/digital_twin -q  # all passed (107)
python demos/open_data_demo_01/run_demo.py                   # PASS PASS

## Git workflow (current — direct clone, no zip)

Git repo: C:\Users\999ye\Downloads\metagenesis-core-public
All edits go directly into this folder.
Push via PR only — main branch is protected (requires CI + PR).

git checkout -b fix/your-branch-name
git add <files>
git commit -m "type: description"
git push origin fix/your-branch-name
# then open PR on GitHub

## Physical Anchor Principle (introduced 2026-03-13)

Two distinct concepts — never conflate:
- Tamper-evident provenance: "was the bundle modified?" → ALL 8 claims
- Physical anchor: "does the number agree with physical reality?" → MTR-1/2/3, DT-FEM-01, DRIFT-01 ONLY

E = 70 GPa for aluminium is a physical constant measured in thousands of labs worldwide.
NOT a chosen threshold. MTR-1 verifies rel_err ≤ 1% vs this constant.
DT-FEM-01 verifies FEM output vs MTR-1 anchor. DRIFT-01 monitors deviation ≤ 5%.

ML_BENCH-01, DATA-PIPE-01, SYSID-01 use chosen convention thresholds — tamper-evident only.
Scope boundary documented in reports/known_faults.yaml :: SCOPE_001.
